steps.title: Draw the consequence
steps.content: Morrison argued that the USGS had left out important considerations, especially the curvature of the earth. The next sentence states the result of that omission: the USGS-determined center was inaccurate.

steps.title: Use a result transition
steps.content: Choice A signals sequence. Choice B signals concession or qualification. Choice D signals contrast. The remaining choice signals that the inaccuracy follows logically from the omitted considerations. The answer is C.
